points: Biodiversity refers to the variety of plants and animals in a region, which are interdependent for food and shelter.
Grouping (classification) is the method of arranging things based on common features to make studying them easier.
Plants are grouped into Herbs (short/tender), Shrubs (medium/woody near ground), and Trees (tall/woody higher up).
Leaf venation can be Reticulate (net-like) or Parallel, while roots are categorized as Taproot (one main) or Fibrous (bunched).
A key correlation exists: Dicots usually have reticulate venation and taproots, while Monocots have parallel venation and fibrous roots.
Animals are grouped based on their movement (crawling, flying, swimming) and habitats.
Habitats are categorized as Terrestrial (land) or Aquatic (water). Amphibians (like frogs) can live in both.
Adaptations are special features (like fleshy desert stems or streamlined fish bodies) that enable survival in specific regions.
Habitat damage leads to biodiversity loss; conservation projects like Project Tiger and community-protected Sacred Groves are essential.
